title: heaviside
summary: Compute the element-wise Heaviside step function.

gpu_support.notes: Keeps real GPU tensors resident when the active provider implements unary_heaviside; otherwise RunMat gathers and evaluates the host implementation.

description: `Y = heaviside(X)` returns the Heaviside step function applied element by element. Negative real values map to `0`, zero maps to `0.5`, and positive values map to `1`.
behaviors: Real scalars, vectors, matrices, and higher-dimensional tensors are evaluated elementwise.
`Inf` maps to `1`, `-Inf` maps to `0`, and `NaN` remains `NaN`.
CPU, provider, WGPU, and fusion paths all use the same real scalar mapping.
Logical inputs are promoted to doubles, so `heaviside(false)` returns `0.5` and `heaviside(true)` returns `1`.
Character arrays are treated as their numeric code points and return doubles of the same shape.
Symbolic scalar inputs return symbolic `heaviside(...)` expressions.
Complex and string inputs are rejected with a stable invalid-input identifier.

examples.description: Evaluate the step at representative scalar values
Gate a sinusoid using a unit step

faqs.question: What value does RunMat use at zero?
faqs.answer: RunMat returns `0.5` for both positive and negative zero, matching MATLAB's convention for numeric Heaviside step values.

faqs.question: Does `heaviside` support complex numbers?
faqs.answer: No. RunMat implements the real-valued Heaviside step function. Complex inputs raise `RunMat:heaviside:InvalidInput` instead of silently choosing a branch.

faqs.question: Does `heaviside` support symbolic expressions?
faqs.answer: Yes. Symbolic scalar inputs remain symbolic and format as `heaviside(expr)`.

faqs.question: Does `heaviside` preserve NaN values?
faqs.answer: Yes. NaN inputs remain NaN on CPU, GPU provider, and fused elementwise paths.

faqs.question: Will `heaviside(gpuArray(...))` stay on the GPU?
faqs.answer: Yes when the active provider implements `unary_heaviside`. Providers without that hook fall back to host evaluation for correctness.

faqs.question: Can `heaviside` participate in fusion?
faqs.answer: Yes. The fusion planner emits an elementwise step expression with explicit zero and NaN handling.

gpu_residency: `heaviside` keeps real GPU tensors resident when the active provider exposes `unary_heaviside`. If that hook is unavailable, RunMat gathers the tensor and evaluates the same scalar mapping on the host.
